bias_defense_notes: 5 MCQs shuffled; position counts: 0→1, 1→1, 2→2, 3→1; max_share=0.40 (≤0.40 — passes). Length-balanced: all 4 options per MCQ within ±20% of mean character length.

questions.objective_ref: Define few-shot prompting and explain the difference between zero-shot, one-shot, and few-shot prompting in plain language.
Identify what makes a good example — correct format, correct tone, and correct level of detail — versus a weak or misleading example.
Write a few-shot prompt with two to four input-output example pairs embedded in the system prompt or user prompt.
Explain why few-shot examples change model output quality and what the model is actually doing when it sees them.
Apply the placement rule — deciding whether examples belong in the system prompt or in the user message — for a given use case.
Define few-shot prompting and explain the difference between zero-shot, one-shot, and few-shot prompting in plain language.
Describe two common mistakes when writing few-shot examples and state how to fix each one.
